vision-sync.input_context: existing vision.md (30 lines) + S3 scenario description + approval gate template
vision-sync.convert_mode_overhead: Evidence Table and Approval Gate sections add ~40% output vs bootstrap create mode

persona-builder.input_context: existing P1.md (25 lines) + vision refinement + new feature requirement
persona-builder.convert_mode_overhead: Relationship to Existing Personas section is convert-mode-only (~15% of output)

feature-discovery.input_context: feature-todo-management.md + enabler-overdue-checker.md + S3 requirement + new persona
feature-discovery.convert_mode_overhead: Cross-reference tables against existing ACs add ~60% output vs bootstrap discovery

writing-spec.input_context: feature-ship-brief + existing feature spec + existing enabler spec + persona P2 + vision refinement
writing-spec.convert_mode_overhead: System Dependencies split into Existing/New tables; cascade diagram; cross-AC references add ~30% vs clean slate

spec-code-sync.input_context: All 4 specs (2 existing + 2 new) + journey J01
spec-code-sync.convert_mode_overhead: Entirely different output structure vs S1; drift detection + conflict check tables are convert-mode-only

journey-sync.input_context: J01 journey + feature-realtime-collab spec + both personas + enabler specs
journey-sync.convert_mode_overhead: Prerequisite reference, conflict analysis table, and cross-journey AC coverage add ~45% vs bootstrap journey

comparison_to_s1.convert_overhead_explanation: Convert mode skills average 41% more tokens per skill than bootstrap mode due to cross-reference validation, drift detection, and approval gates. Total is lower because fewer skills are exercised in an iteration.
